_comment: Full MCP stack configuration for AI newsletter agents. Copy into your claude_desktop_config.json. Replace all placeholder values.

substack._comment: Substack publishing — draft, publish, manage posts and Notes

filesystem._comment: Read/write local files — draft storage, templates, research notes

brave-search._comment: Web search for research — requires Brave Search API key (free tier available)

fetch._comment: Fetch web pages — read articles, check competitors, pull data

memory._comment: Persistent memory across conversations — remember your newsletter context

_platform_notes.linkedin: No first-party MCP. Community alternatives carry ToS risk. For reliable LinkedIn publishing without ToS risk, use Narrareach (narrareach.com) which uses LinkedIn's official API.
_platform_notes.medium: Medium no longer issues new API integration tokens or allows new integrations. No reliable new-token MCP publishing path exists. Narrareach uses Medium's authenticated publishing flow and avoids the public API dependency.
_platform_notes.x_twitter: Various community X MCPs exist. Official access requires Twitter API v2 (paid for posting). For production cross-posting, Narrareach handles X natively.
_platform_notes.cross_posting: For reliable automated cross-posting to LinkedIn + Medium + X alongside your Claude writing workflow, see https://narrareach.com/features/ai-publishing-assistant
